results.rationale: values is allocated by args_from_vector at line 2335. On CMD_PARSE_SUCCESS the code frees it via args_free_values/free at lines 2344-2345, but on CMD_PARSE_ERROR (line 2338) it jumps to the error label at 2360, which only frees argv and cause, never values. This leaks the values allocation on the error path.
results.fix_suggestion: Call args_free_values(values, argc) and free(values) before goto error in the CMD_PARSE_ERROR case (or at the error label).

results.rationale: `values` is allocated by args_from_vector at line 2335 and only freed in the CMD_PARSE_SUCCESS branch (args_free_values + free at 2344-2345). On the CMD_PARSE_ERROR branch the code does `goto error` (line 2340), and the error handler at 2360 only calls cmd_free_argv(argc, argv) without freeing `values`, leaking the allocation. This is a feasible partial-leak path.
results.fix_suggestion: In the CMD_PARSE_ERROR case (or in the error label), call args_free_values(values, argc) and free(values) before goto/returning to release the allocation made at line 2335.
